title: lappa major subsp. pubens
intro: Lappa major subsp. pubens, also known as Nabalus albus, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Lappa major subsp. pubens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
